text: I keep studying because I love to travel, so English opens doors.
Although it is not easy, I never want to stop.
Reason
Cause
Effect
Contrast
Link
Despite
Nevertheless
On the other hand
I study English because I love to travel.
I love to travel, so I study English.
Although it is hard, I never stop.
It is hard; however, I never stop.
Sandra, what is the main reason you keep studying English?
I study because I love to travel, so English opens many doors for me.
That is a strong reason. But is anything difficult about it?
Although the grammar is hard, I never stop. It is tiring; however, the class gives me energy.
Wonderful. So what is the effect of all this practice?
The effect is that my confidence grows every week. On the other hand, if I stopped, I would lose all of that.
The main reason is
I chose this because
Although it is hard,
It is useful, so I practice every day.
It is tiring; however, I love it.
I love my city. On the other hand, it is crowded.

Let me explain why I started learning a new language so late in life. I chose to study Italian because my grandmother came from a small town near Rome. I wanted to read her old letters, so I signed up for a class. At first it was very hard, although I never thought about quitting. The grammar was difficult; however, the people in my class were kind and patient. I made a lot of mistakes, but every mistake taught me something. As a result, after one year, I could finally read those letters. I am not fluent, and yet I feel proud. The reason is simple. I did it for love, not for work. So the lesson is this: find a reason that matters to you, and the effort becomes a pleasure. Although the road is long, a strong reason makes every step lighter.

I want to explain why I keep studying English, even though I am fifty-one. Some people think it is too late, however I completely disagree. I study because the world feels bigger when I can talk to more people. I love to travel, so English opens many doors for me. The classes are not always easy; nevertheless, I look forward to each one. Sometimes I am tired after work, but I still join the lesson, because it gives me energy. As a result, my confidence grows a little every week. I make mistakes, and yet I keep going. The main reason is curiosity. I am curious about other cultures, other ideas, other ways of living. So although progress is slow, the effect on my life is huge. On the other hand, if I stopped now, I would lose all of that. So I will not stop.

First, say your main idea, for example I keep studying English. Then, to give the reason, add because and the cause, like because I love to travel. Next, to show the effect, use so or as a result, for example so I can talk to more people. After that, to add a contrast, use although or however, like although it is not always easy. Finally, end with a calm conclusion, so your listener understands both your reason and your contrast.
